market.advice: Breadth is healthy and broad: 71% of stocks sit above their 50-day MA, VIX is calm at 15.9 and falling, and SPY closed above its 50-day ($736.75) at $751.28, up 3.1% over the past week (QQQ also above its 50-day). This is a defensives-and-financials-led advance -- regional and money-center banks (BK, NTRS, CFG, FITB, STT, GS), insurers (AFL, MET, TRV, AIZ), utilities (AEP, D, DTE) and REITs (TRNO, PSA, O) dominate the cleanest setups, with select tech (FTNT, GRMN, AAPL, AMD) and industrials (HWM, UNP, GE) confirming underneath. Normal position sizing is appropriate given the low-VIX, broad-breadth backdrop, but several names are technically overbought after the run (GE RSI 90.9, SIRI RSI 88.7, PGNY RSI 85.7, TRV RSI 83.2, ILMN RSI 76.6) -- favor the less-extended STRONG BUY names or wait for a pullback on the stretched ones rather than chasing. TradingView's independent technical rating agrees with most of the scan's leaders, but flags real dissent on a handful of semiconductor/tech names the quant score likes: Corning (GLW) is outright SELL-rated on TV (7 buy/10 sell) despite a textbook VCP, and Applied Materials (AMAT) and Micron (MU) both come back NEUTRAL/split on TV oscillators even though they score as quant STRONG BUYs -- treat those three as WATCH, not chase. The overhanging tail risk is geopolitical: tankers have been hit near the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran-US tension and Khamenei's funeral, oil ticked up on the news, and ECB's Schnabel says "the Iran shock is not over" -- not yet showing up in VIX, but worth monitoring for energy/oil-price spillover into risk assets. Regional-bank and money-center earnings begin landing over the next 7-15 days across several of today's top picks (BK, GS, CFG, FITB, STT) -- a real catalyst window.
